message:
feat(render-svg): add adaptive detail tiers, print-optimized CSS, and label truncation

Implement three-tier detail rendering system (Compact/Normal/Rich) that
adapts SVG output based on diagram area or explicit configuration. In
Compact mode, edge labels and cluster labels are hidden, node labels are
truncated to 20 chars (or hidden entirely for very small diagrams), and
shadows are disabled. Normal mode applies moderate truncation limits
(48 chars for nodes, 40 for edges) with slightly reduced font sizes.
Rich mode preserves all labels at full fidelity.

Add Auto tier selection that automatically chooses based on layout area
thresholds: <56k px^2 -> Compact, <220k -> Normal, otherwise Rich.
Emit data-detail-tier attribute on the SVG root for downstream tooling.

Add SvgRenderConfig fields: detail_tier (MermaidTier), min_font_size
(floor for readability), and print_optimized (boolean).

Generate @media print CSS rules that enforce minimum font sizes, high-
contrast stroke colors, and white cluster fills for clean printed output.

Fix XML escaping: stop escaping '>' to '>' which was breaking CSS
child combinators (div > p) when SVG is embedded inline in HTML5. The
'>' character only needs escaping inside CDATA end markers (]]>) per
the XML spec.

Fix cylinder shape arc direction: change the bottom arc from
(false, true) to (false, false) so the closing curve mirrors the top
arc correctly.

Fix hyperlink rendering: flatten nested if-let blocks into a single
let-chain expression for cleaner code.

Adjust theme whitespace formatting (no functional change).

Add tests for detail tier behavior (compact hides labels, rich preserves
them, auto marks data attribute), print CSS embedding, and edge label
truncation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
